workiqQueriesRun: What email threads did I send or receive most frequently in the past 7 days? List subject patterns, sender/recipient groups, and approximate time spent.
Are there recurring email types I write regularly like status updates, approvals, scheduling requests, or data requests? Show examples from the past week.
Which emails required the most back-and-forth (most replies in a thread) this past week, and what was the topic?
What recurring meetings did I attend this past week? For each, what was the typical agenda type and who attends?
How much total time did I spend in meetings this past week, broken down by meeting type (1:1, team sync, external, etc.)?
Which meeting types happen every week at roughly the same time with the same people?
What Teams channels or chats am I most active in over the past 7 days? What topics come up repeatedly?
Are there questions I get asked repeatedly in Teams chats things people regularly come to me for?
What types of information do I most frequently share or look up in Teams conversations this week?
What types of documents did I create, edit, or review most often this past week? Any recurring doc types like reports, specs, or decks?
Are there documents I update on a regular cadence (weekly/monthly reports, trackers, dashboards)?
What SharePoint sites or OneDrive folders do I access most frequently?
Across email, meetings, and Teams, what topics or projects consumed the most of my time this week?
Are there workflows that seem to repeat where I do the same sequence of actions (e.g., get email -> schedule meeting -> create doc)?
What tasks do multiple people on my team seem to do independently that could be standardized?

signals.title: Azure DevOps Work Item Notification Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: By far the largest cluster in the inbox for the period, spanning dozens of distinct messages across multiple Teams, Copilot, Eval Infra, CMD, and Partner areas. Subject patterns: Task ###### ..., Bug ###### ..., Requirement ###### .... Dozens of notifications daily from azuredevops@microsoft.com.

signals.title: CAPE Customer Evaluation Discussion Threads
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple long-form, multi-recipient threads with substantive content and attachments, recurring across the week. Subject patterns: Re: CAPE East Project Crucible, Re: Customer donated evals added Playbook, Announcing EvalCon Register + propose content.

signals.title: Loop Workspace Collaboration Invitations
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject: You have been looped in! You invited team members to collaborate in the Skilluminator Workspace and other projects. Part of a recurring collaboration pattern bringing stakeholders into shared workspaces rather than long back-and-forth email threads.

signals.title: Security and Compliance Alert Handling
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple alerts clustered in the same day. Subject patterns: Action Required: File Blocked, open-source repo permission changes, CISO / Purview / Compliance notifications. From: Office365 no-reply, Microsoft Digital Compliance, Open Source Notifications.

signals.title: Microsoft Viva and Org Digest Processing
signals.rawExcerpt: Regular but non-interactive emails. Subject patterns: Microsoft Daily Digest, Microsoft Weekly Digest, Viva Engage community announcements, BIC All Hands reminders. Low attention per email, high cumulative attention cost.

signals.title: Customer Eval Governance Multi-Stakeholder Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread Customer donated evals added Playbook shows multiple replies among several stakeholders with clarifications and follow-ups rather than a single announcement. The conversation clearly evolved over multiple messages, indicating sustained discussion and coordination.

signals.title: External Content-Sharing Readiness Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread Atlas Session Pre-Check: includes an external request, an internal clarification, and follow-up responses looping in additional stakeholders. Multiple replies addressing scope, readiness, and permissions — meaningful back-and-forth rather than a one-way update.

signals.title: Urgent Cross-Team Technical Escalation via Email
signals.rawExcerpt: Thread URGENT: DBS Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites: urgency marker, large recipient list, repeated RE: subject line — active multi-reply thread with coordination and escalation across teams about a complex architecture issue.

signals.title: US Pod Weekly Stand-Up
signals.rawExcerpt: US Pod Stand Up: Every Tuesday, 12:35-1:00 PM. Classic weekly stand-up with a consistent pod roster. Same required attendees, stable weekly cadence.

signals.title: Weekly Project Crucible V-Team Execution Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Meeting: Every Monday, 8:05-8:30 AM. Clear weekly project execution sync with stable required attendees. Eval gaps, DA/MCS evals, CI/CD integration, documentation needs discussed regularly.

signals.title: CAPE Architecture Weekly Call
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Architecture Call: Every Monday, 9:05-10:00 AM. Weekly architecture forum with a stable core audience. Mondays at ~9 AM are heavily contested across CAPE, signaling an org-wide coordination hotspot.

signals.title: CAPE Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Weekly Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE / Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync: Every Friday, 10:00-11:00 AM. Weekly cross-team coordination with consistent leadership presence.

signals.title: Friday Learning and Scaling Deep-Work Block
signals.rawExcerpt: Learning and Scaling Fridays PST: Every Friday, 2:05-5:00 PM. Same small set of required attendees, long block, consistent weekly learning cadence. Fridays stack both broadcast (Friday Show) and deep-work learning back-to-back.

signals.title: The Friday Show Org-Wide Broadcast Meeting
signals.rawExcerpt: The Friday Show!: Every Friday, 8:05-9:00 AM. Highly consistent weekly slot with the same large audience, broad CAPE org distribution lists plus recurring leaders. More broadcast than working session.

signals.title: CAPE Camp AIR Multi-Day Training Workshop
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Camp AIR: two ~9-hour all-day events on Monday and Tuesday plus adjacent sessions (Mindset, Workflow, Environment Prep, M365 Copilot Productivity, D365/Dataverse, Standups, EOD Debriefs). This clearly dominated the week in raw time spent, accounting for multiple full workdays.

signals.title: Eval Strategy Consulting from Field and FDEs
signals.rawExcerpt: Single most recurring Teams theme: people frequently ask how many evals are enough per scenario, whether they are over- or under-testing, how to think about coverage vs. effort tradeoffs. You are widely seen as the person who translates eval theory into practical, defensible guidance.

signals.title: Eval Metrics and Pass Rate Customer-Safe Framing
signals.rawExcerpt: People repeatedly ask what pass rate is reasonable vs. unrealistic, whether 100% accuracy is the right goal. Direct questions like How have you been talking about this with customers? They want your phrasing, not just the answer. You are being asked for customer-safe narratives.

signals.title: Eval Artifact and Repository Sharing
signals.rawExcerpt: You frequently shared GitHub repositories (eval scenario libraries, skill agents, playbooks), PowerPoint decks and SharePoint files for field enablement and roadmaps, Loop pages and collaborative docs. You often respond to questions by anchoring discussions in shared artifacts, not just opinions.

signals.title: Skill Detector and Skill Creator Asset Distribution
signals.rawExcerpt: Tight cluster of conversations on skill detector and skill creator assets, Skilluminator demos during Camp AIR, tooling bridging Work IQ into agent capabilities. You shared skill detectors, YAML/MD files, and ZIPs repeatedly across multiple chat threads.

signals.title: Customer VoC and ADO Work Item Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: You often looked up or referenced specific customer issues: checking for duplicate customer asks, discussing ADO work items, incident IDs, and escalation status, coordinating triage or follow-ups with TPMs and engineers. Your Teams usage reflects active customer-facing accountability.

signals.title: EvalCon Event Planning and Content Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: Active Teams discussion about EvalCon onsite planning, deadlines, submission timing. Coordination about topic ownership, process vs tool framing, submission deadline pressure. A future-dated event but already a meaningful slice of this week's thinking and coordination time.

signals.title: Loop Collaborative Notes for PM Reviews and Status Tracking
signals.rawExcerpt: You interacted heavily with Loop files for PM reviews and working sessions (MCS Analytics and Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review, Evals VoC Current Status, Top eval tenants, Builder PM Demos). These are consistently reviewed, edited, and re-shared. Loop is your primary medium for cross-functional alignment, meeting notes, and status tracking.

signals.title: PowerPoint Deck Review and Alignment for Leadership
signals.rawExcerpt: You reviewed or edited multiple .pptx files: monthly/exec reviews (2026-03 Monthly Engineering Review), strategy decks (AgentArchetypeFramework, ESS_Evals_R+R), event/field-facing decks (CAPE Day CE Lighthouse). Recurring cadence of reviewing decks for alignment, storytelling, or leadership consumption.

signals.title: Solution Discovery Reports for Customers
signals.rawExcerpt: You engaged with several .docx files including Solution Discovery Reports (Fidelity SDR, TDD Tributary Briefings Agent SDR) and product specs (Language Support in Agent Evaluation, Read-Only Analytics Access One pager). Written narrative docs and specs are a core part of your week.

signals.title: Agent Eval Progress Report Weekly Cadence Update
signals.rawExcerpt: Agent_Eval_Progress_Report_v4.docx: a dated progress report (March 11, 2026) with you listed as owner, modified recently, indicating an ongoing reporting cadence. Together with Agent_Eval_4Week_Workplan.docx (deliverables tracker with owners, due dates, status fields), strongly suggests a weekly or bi-weekly eval progress + planning cadence.

signals.title: HTML Dashboard Refresh and Distribution
signals.rawExcerpt: dashboard.html (Skilluminator Cycle 3 Dashboard) explicitly labeled by cycle and week (Week of 2026-03-16), recently updated and shared. CAPE_Eval_Dashboard.html updated and shared multiple times on the same date across Teams threads, consistent with a dashboard that gets refreshed and redistributed during enablement or reporting moments.

signals.title: MCS Customer Signals Tracker for Planning
signals.rawExcerpt: MCS Customer Signals for Planning.xlsx: a signal aggregation and prioritization tracker used for planning, frequently shared in planning threads. Also: Agent Eval Sets_Template.xlsx repeatedly shared and reused across multiple dates and contexts as part of a recurring eval workflow.

signals.title: Email-to-Meeting-to-Notes PM Workflow Loop
signals.rawExcerpt: Long recap emails with structured Agenda / Demos / Decisions / Follow-up Tasks after meetings. Loop files created or shared immediately after meetings to capture notes and decisions. Implicit workflow: Trigger email or open question - Schedule meeting - Run meeting - Create shared notes - Extract decisions + action items - Send recap.

signals.title: PRD Authoring with Parallel Competing Templates
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple distinct PRD templates and formats are actively used across the org: a Clean PRD template, another PRD template with different sections and sign-off expectations, and wiki guidance. These coexist rather than reference a single canonical template. Different PMs independently decide which sections matter.

signals.title: Leadership Status Update Authoring Fragmented Multi-Author
signals.rawExcerpt: Meetings explicitly discuss weekly status updates, bi-weekly leadership newsletters, manual reminders for DRIs to submit updates. Active effort to automate reminders and consolidate updates implies the current process is fragmented. Multiple people independently writing updates in different formats.

signals.title: Enablement Deck Proliferation Across Teams
signals.rawExcerpt: Explicit effort to consolidate many existing decks into a single master deck. Discussion acknowledges multiple overlapping resources, pitch decks, and presentations already exist and are being used in parallel. PMs and leads independently creating slides for similar audiences, maintaining their own versions of core messaging.
